steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers conclude that deceptive display prevalence is mediated by absolute latitude of brooding sites, independent of avian order. Supporting evidence must show the percentage of species using displays rising or falling consistently with latitude — across orders, not just within one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '54% brood at 50°–80° but most display-users brood at 0°–30°': this shows where birds brood, not what percentage use displays at each latitude band. It cannot confirm that latitude drives display prevalence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Charadriiformes use displays across all latitudes; other orders only at latitudes 10°–20° higher than typical': display prevalence varies by order, not uniformly by latitude. This directly undermines the "independent of avian order" part of the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Charadriiformes prevalence is independent of latitude; other orders limited to 0°–30°': again, the pattern differs by order rather than following a single latitude-driven trend, contradicting the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice D — '34% of species at 0°–30° use displays; 60% at 50°–80° use displays': across all orders in the study, display prevalence rises with absolute latitude (34% $\Rightarrow$ 60%). This directly shows that latitude, not order, drives how common the trait is. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
